market_context.diagnostic_focus: rebound environment, frontcourt competition, and minute-to-board conversion

phase_1_game_phase_analysis.summary: Overtime-level minutes and heavy miss volume created a strong overall rebound environment, but Minnesota's board share split more toward Gobert and Naz Reid than Julius Randle. Houston's wing-glass path for Amen remained active throughout.
key_distribution_notes: Houston rebounds distributed across Amen Thompson (9), Jabari Smith Jr. (12), Tari Eason (8), Reed Sheppard (8), and Sengun (6).
Minnesota rebound concentration leaned into Rudy Gobert (14) and Naz Reid (13), reducing Julius Randle access.
Randle logged 42 minutes but still finished with only 6 rebounds, signaling role-share suppression rather than simple minute loss.

notes: Gobert posted 14 rebounds with 7 offensive boards, absorbing a major share of Minnesota's center-lane opportunities.
Naz Reid's 13 rebounds off the bench created an additional frontcourt siphon effect on Randle's ceiling.
Amen Thompson maintained elite glass activity with 6 offensive rebounds, validating the high-motor wing rebound pathway.
Minnesota's size rotation created internal competition for rebounds, especially in a game with Gobert and Reid both highly active on the glass.
Randle's offensive volume was strong, but his rebound role was secondary to the true bigs in this specific environment.
Amen's transition activity, motor, and weak-side crashing remained live despite Houston's balanced frontcourt usage.

hits: Amen Thompson over 7.5 rebounds was a correct environmental and archetype-based read.
Houston wing rebound upside remained strong in a high-miss, high-minute game.
misses: Julius Randle over 6.5 rebounds missed due to Gobert-Reid rebound crowding.
Model likely overweighted minutes and underweighted same-team frontcourt competition.
patch_recommendations: Downgrade PF rebound overs when paired with an elite center plus an active rebounding bench big.
Add same-team frontcourt crowding logic when two teammates project for 10+ rebound chances each.
Boost versatile crash wings like Amen when game environment supports extra offensive rebounding volume.

final_verdict.overall: Good environment read, but incomplete role-share adjustment on the Minnesota side.
final_verdict.confidence_takeaway: Keep leaning into Amen rebound overs in high-chaos environments, but be more careful with Randle overs when Gobert and Naz Reid are both active and projecting strong board share.
